lifecycles._note: Light, VPC-FREE, COVERAGE-ONLY. Closes the 7 SCF write endpoints the base compute-scf-cloud-function-cronjob-trigger lifecycle deliberately skips: the privatelink-ENDPOINT family (create/approve/connect/request/delete), updateprivatelinkservice (PUT .../privatelink-services), and the apigateway TRIGGER (POST /v1/triggers/apigateway). The base lifecycle's _note documents WHY PL is excluded there: enabling a privatelink-service strands the function in 'Creating' so the in-lifecycle teardown 400s with CloudFunctionNotDeletableError. So here NOTHING is provisioned — {cloud_function_id}/{endpoint_id} are SOFT-seeded from list GETs (NO-OP selectors that find nothing) so they stay LITERAL at runtime -> a non-existent '{cloud_function_id}' yields a guaranteed 404 and the PL/trigger writes NEVER touch a real function, while the catalog still normalizes {..} to '*' so every endpoint is RECORDED (covered). Every write is optional+grouped with broad expect_status [200,201,202,400,403,404,409,422] so a 4xx/404 from a non-existent id never fails the run. createprivatelinkendpoint's own POST captures {endpoint_id} when it (improbably) succeeds, in which case its DELETE cleans up. apigateway trigger needs a real api_gateway/resource/method id we don't own -> 4xx expected. Bodies docs/best-effort, UNPROVEN (no api_bodies.json entries for the PL family / apigateway trigger exist).

steps.name: seed-placeholders
steps._note: COST/SAFETY: declares {cloud_function_id}/{endpoint_id} via NO-OP soft-capture selectors that deliberately find nothing, so at runtime these placeholders stay LITERAL. The URL then carries a non-existent literal id -> guaranteed 404 (never mutates a real cloud-function's PL config — which would strand it in 'Creating' and block teardown, exactly the failure the base lifecycle's _note warns about), while the catalog still normalizes {..} to '*' so every endpoint is RECORDED. create-privatelink-endpoint captures its own {endpoint_id} on success.

steps.name: create-apigateway-trigger
steps._note: UNPROVEN body. POST /v1/triggers/apigateway attaches an API-gateway trigger to a function. Needs a real api_gateway_id + resource/method that we don't own -> 4xx expected. cloud_function_id soft-seeded or literal -> records the endpoint either way. No api_bodies.json entry; fields guessed from the cronjob-trigger shape + apigateway naming.

steps.name: update-privatelink-service
steps._note: UNPROVEN body. PUT /v1/cloud-functions/{cloud_function_id}/configurations/privatelink-services enables/configures the function's privatelink SERVICE (distinct from the per-endpoint family below). enable/disable was REMOVED from the base lifecycle because enabling strands the function in 'Creating'; here cloud_function_id is literal -> 404, so it is recorded WITHOUT enabling PL on a real function. Body guessed (enabled flag + allowed account/project list).

steps.name: create-privatelink-endpoint
steps._note: UNPROVEN body. POST /v1/cloud-functions/{cloud_function_id}/configurations/privatelink-endpoints requests a privatelink endpoint into a consumer subnet (synthetic subnet/endpoint-service ids -> 4xx). cloud_function_id literal -> 404. Captures {endpoint_id} on the off chance of success so the matching DELETE in this group tears it down.

steps.name: request-privatelink-endpoint
steps._note: UNPROVEN body. PUT .../privatelink-endpoints/{endpoint_id}/request (re)submits the endpoint connection request. endpoint_id soft-seeded or literal -> 404; records the endpoint.

steps.name: approve-privatelink-endpoint
steps._note: UNPROVEN body. PUT .../privatelink-endpoints/{endpoint_id}/approval approves a pending endpoint request (provider side). literal endpoint_id -> 404; records the endpoint.

steps.name: connect-privatelink-endpoint
steps._note: UNPROVEN body. PUT .../privatelink-endpoints/{endpoint_id}/connection connects/disconnects an approved endpoint. literal endpoint_id -> 404; records the endpoint.

steps.name: delete-privatelink-endpoint
steps._note: DELETE .../privatelink-endpoints/{endpoint_id}. Also the cleanup for create-privatelink-endpoint; runs here explicitly so the endpoint-delete endpoint is recorded even when the create 404s (literal endpoint_id -> 404 still records).
